mission.title: Install and Configure Open WebUI
mission.description: Open WebUI is a user-friendly AI platform designed for self-hosting various AI models, including Ollama and OpenAI APIs. It provides an extensible interface and supports offline operation, making it ideal for users seeking a customizable AI deployment solution. Key trade-offs include the need for local infrastructure and potential complexity in setup compared to managed services.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the Open WebUI CLI to manage your deployment.

```bash

curl -Lo open-webui-cli https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui/releases/download/v0.8.10/open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x open-webui-cli && sudo mv open-webui-cli /usr/local/bin/

```
commands: curl -Lo open-webui-cli https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui/releases/download/v0.8.10/open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x open-webui-cli && sudo mv open-webui-cli /usr/local/bin/

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Set up a new Kubernetes cluster with the required version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create open-webui-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your kubeconfig to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials open-webui-cluster --region=us-central1

```

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

steps.title: Install Open WebUI
steps.description:
Deploy Open WebUI using Helm with the specified version.

```bash

helm repo add open-webui https://charts.openwebui.com && helm repo update

helm install open-webui open-webui/open-webui --version 0.8.10 --namespace open-webui --create-namespace

```
commands: helm repo add open-webui https://charts.openwebui.com && helm repo update

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for your Open WebUI deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/open-webui/open-webui/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/open-webui/open-webui/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

uninstall.title: Uninstall Open WebUI
uninstall.description:
Remove Open WebUI and all associated resources. This will result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall open-webui --namespace open-webui

kubectl delete namespace open-webui

```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Open WebUI
upgrade.description:
Backup your current deployment, perform the upgrade, and verify the installation.

```bash

kubectl cp open-webui-pod:/data /backup/open-webui-data

helm upgrade open-webui open-webui/open-webui --version 0.8.10 --namespace open-webui

kubectl get pods -n open-webui

```

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image version may not exist or is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify the image version in your Helm chart and ensure it matches the available tags.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may not be starting due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and adjust the configuration or resource requests.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be exposed correctly or is not running.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service is running and check the service configuration with 'kubectl get services'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the deployment.

**Fix:**
Scale up your cluster or adjust the resource requests in your deployment configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Ollama models, improved API integration for OpenAI-compatible services.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required when upgrading from previous versions.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get pods -n open-webui' showing all pods in the 'Running' state.

helm repo add open-webui https://charts.openwebui.com && helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
